Class HttpExecutor

java.lang.Object
com.habbashx.executors.Executor
com.habbashx.executors.HttpExecutor
public class HttpExecutor extends Executor
Concrete Executor that dispatches HTTP requests via RequestRegistry and deserializes the raw JSON response using a JsonSerializer.

Supports both synchronous and asynchronous execution:

  • If returnType is CompletableFuture<T>, the request is sent asynchronously and the inner type T is used for deserialization.
  • Otherwise, the request is sent synchronously and the full returnType is used.

Any exception from the synchronous path is wrapped in a HttpException.

  • Constructor Details

    • HttpExecutor

      public HttpExecutor(RequestRegistry registry, JsonSerializer serializer)
      Parameters:
      registry - the request registry responsible for HTTP dispatch
      serializer - the JSON serializer used to deserialize the response body

  • Method Details

    • execute

      public Object execute(MethodContext context, Type returnType)
      Executes the HTTP request and returns a deserialized response.

      If returnType is CompletableFuture<T>, the request is sent asynchronously. Otherwise it is sent synchronously and any failure is wrapped in a HttpException.

      Specified by:
      execute in class Executor
      Parameters:
      context - fully-resolved request context from the interceptor pipeline
      returnType - the target type for deserialization; may be parameterized or a future
      Returns:
      the deserialized object, or a CompletableFuture wrapping it for async calls
      Throws:
      HttpException - if the synchronous HTTP call fails
